response.justification: The candidate is a strong generalist engineer with impressive breadth across web, mobile, and low-latency systems, but he does not meet the role's core mandate: his only professional C++ experience is a roughly seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015, versus the required 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His financial-services exposure (RIDE Capital) was TypeScript/Node-based investment tooling, not fixed income or capital markets C++ work, and there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle experience. The gap on the mandatory skills is too large to justify an interview for this specific engagement.
strengths: Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (DLang async I/O services handling millions of requests per second)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus DevOps and tooling experience
Excellent communication skills, demonstrated through workshops, conference talks, and courses
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience (2014-2015), far short of the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No fixed income, derivatives, or capital markets domain expertise; fintech exposure was a Node/TypeScript investment platform
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Missing fixed income / capital markets domain depth
Strong general engineering and low-latency systems background
